contents: Require Import Extensions HFiber Truncations NullHomotopy Limits.Pullback.

(** We construct "canonical" binary meets of reflective subuniverses (that is, whose underlying subuniverse is an intersection), without assuming accessibility.  In particular, we will show:

1. Given two reflective subuniverses L and O, if [L O X] is [O]-modal, then it is a reflection into the canonical meet.  In particular, this is always the case if [L] preserves [O]-modal types; this is Theorem 3.30 of RSS.

1. If L and O are lex modalities satisfying an additional "composability" condition, then the composite functor [L o O] converges to a reflection into the canonical meet after n+2 applications when applied to an n-type for some finite n.

The latter gives in particular a synthetic approach to higher sheafification (stack completion).  As described at https://ncatlab.org/nlab/show/plus+construction+on+presheaves, for any site C the topos of presheaves on its Grothendieck topology is cohesive and even totally connected, so that its shape and sharp modalities are both lex.  Their canonical meet is the topos of sheaves for the given topology, and the composite functor [shape o sharp] is the usual "plus construction" on (higher) presheaves.  Thus, we recover synthetically the result that an n-truncated type can be stackified by (n+2) applications of the plus construction.  We also refer to [L o O] as a "plus construction" in the general case of reflective subuniverses.  *)
(** The canonical meet of two subuniverses is their intersection. *)

goals.conclusion: Subuniverse
Subuniverse
Type -> Type
Funext -> forall T : Type, IsHProp (?In_internal T)

forall T U : Type,
?In_internal T -> forall f : T -> U, IsEquiv f -> ?In_internal U
Type -> Type

contents: intros X; exact (In L X * In O X).

goals.conclusion: Funext -> forall T : Type, IsHProp ((fun X : Type => In L X * In O X) T)

forall T U : Type,
(fun X : Type => In L X * In O X) T ->
forall f : T -> U, IsEquiv f -> (fun X : Type => In L X * In O X) U

contents: intros T U [? ?] f feq; split; exact (inO_equiv_inO _ f).
#[export] Instance inO_inmeet_l (X : Type) `{im : In Meet X} : In L X := fst im.
#[export] Instance inO_inmeet_r (X : Type) `{im : In Meet X} : In O X := snd im.
(** The basic tool in studying its reflectivity is the "plus construction" that applies the two reflectors in sequence. *)
#[export] Instance inO_plus_l (X : Type) : In L (Plus X) := _.
(** This is not necessarily a reflector, but it is a well-pointed endofunctor. *)

Definition to_plus (X : Type) : X -> Plus X
    := to L (O X) o to O X.

Definition plus_functor {X Y : Type} (f : X -> Y) : Plus X -> Plus Y
    := O_functor L (O_functor O f).

Definition to_plus_natural {X Y : Type} (f : X -> Y)
    : plus_functor f o to_plus X == to_plus Y o f.

goals.conclusion: plus_functor f o to_plus X == to_plus Y o f
plus_functor f o to_plus X == to_plus Y o f
plus_functor f (to_plus X x) = to_plus Y (f x)

O_functor L (O_functor O f) (to L (O X) (to O X x)) =
to L (O Y) (to O Y (f x))

contents: refine (to_O_natural L (O_functor O f) (to O X x) @ _).

goals.conclusion: to L (O Y) (O_functor O f (to O X x)) = to L (O Y) (to O Y (f x))
O_functor O f (to O X x) = to O Y (f x)

contents:
Definition wellpointed_plus (X : Type)
    : to_plus (Plus X) == plus_functor (to_plus X).

goals.conclusion: to_plus (Plus X) == plus_functor (to_plus X)
to_plus (Plus X) == plus_functor (to_plus X)

(fun x : O X => to_plus (Plus X) (to L (O X) x)) ==
(fun x : O X => plus_functor (to_plus X) (to L (O X) x))
to_plus (Plus X) (to L (O X) ox) = plus_functor (to_plus X) (to L (O X) ox)

to L (O (Plus X)) (to O (Plus X) (to L (O X) ox)) =
O_functor L (O_functor O (fun x : X => to L (O X) (to O X x)))
  (to L (O X) ox)

to L (O (Plus X)) (to O (Plus X) (to L (O X) ox)) =
to L (O (Plus X)) (O_functor O (fun x : X => to L (O X) (to O X x)) ox)

to O (Plus X) (to L (O X) ox) =
O_functor O (fun x : X => to L (O X) (to O X x)) ox

contents: revert ox; apply O_indpaths; intros x.

goals.conclusion:
to O (Plus X) (to L (O X) (to O X x)) =
O_functor O (fun x0 : X => to L (O X) (to O X x0)) (to O X x)

contents: (** Moreover, it has the desired factorization property of a reflector (though it may not belong to the meet subuniverse itself). *)

Definition ooextendable_plus {X Y : Type} `{In Meet Y}
    : ooExtendableAlong (to_plus X) (fun _  => Y).

goals.conclusion: ooExtendableAlong (to_plus X) (fun _ : Plus X => Y)
ooExtendableAlong (to_plus X) (fun _ : Plus X => Y)

contents:
apply (ooextendable_compose _ (to O X) (to L (O X)));
      rapply extendable_to_O.

Definition plus_rec {P Q : Type} `{In Meet Q} (f : P -> Q)
    : Plus P -> Q
    := (fst (ooextendable_plus 1%nat) f).1.

Definition plus_rec_beta {P Q : Type} `{In Meet Q} (f : P -> Q) (x : P)
    : plus_rec f (to_plus P x) = f x
    := (fst (ooextendable_plus 1%nat) f).2 x.

Definition plus_indpaths {P Q : Type} `{In Meet Q} (g h : Plus P -> Q)
             (p : g o to_plus P == h o to_plus P)
    : g == h
    := (fst (snd (ooextendable_plus 2%nat) g h) p).1.

Definition plus_indpaths_beta {P Q : Type} `{In Meet Q} (g h : Plus P -> Q)
             (p : g o (to_plus P) == h o (to_plus P)) (x : P)
    : plus_indpaths g h p (to_plus P x) = p x
    := (fst (snd (ooextendable_plus 2%nat) g h) p).2 x.
(** Moreover, its fixed points, as a pointed endofunctor, are the types in the meet. *)
Definition isequiv_plus_inmeet (X : Type) `{In Meet X} : IsEquiv (to_plus X).

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (to_plus X)
IsEquiv (to_plus X)

contents: (** Recalling that a type is connected for a reflective subuniverse if and only if its reflector is nullhomotopic, we define a type to be "plus-connected" if its map to plus is nullhomotopic.  If the meet is reflective, this coincides with connectedness for that reflective subuniverse. *)

Definition plusconnected_equiv {X Y : Type} (f : X <~> Y)
    : PlusConnected X -> PlusConnected Y.

goals.conclusion: PlusConnected X -> PlusConnected Y
PlusConnected X -> PlusConnected Y
PlusConnected Y

hypotheses.type: forall x : X, to_plus X x = px

goals.conclusion: to_plus Y y = plus_functor f px

hypotheses.type: forall x : X, to_plus X x = px

goals.conclusion: to_plus Y y = plus_functor f (to_plus X (f^-1 y))

hypotheses.type: forall x : X, to_plus X x = px

goals.conclusion: to_plus Y y = to_plus Y (f (f^-1 y))

hypotheses.type: forall x : X, to_plus X x = px

contents: (** Similarly, we say a map is plus-connected if all of its fibers are. *)
Definition PlusConnMap {X Y : Type} (f : X -> Y) := forall y, PlusConnected (hfiber f y).
(** Let's now assume we are trying to intersect two lex modalities. *)
(** The plus construction, being a composite of two lex functors, is also lex.  Thus, it preserves path-types. *)

Definition plus_path {X : Type} (x y : X)
    : Plus L O (x = y) <~> (to_plus L O X x = to_plus L O X y).

goals.conclusion: Plus L O (x = y) <~> to_plus L O X x = to_plus L O X y
Plus L O (x = y) <~> to_plus L O X x = to_plus L O X y

contents: refine (equiv_path_O L (to O X x) (to O X y) oE _).

goals.conclusion: Plus L O (x = y) <~> L (to O X x = to O X y)
O (x = y) <~> to O X x = to O X y

contents:
Definition plus_path_to_plus {X : Type} (x y : X)
    : plus_path x y o to_plus L O (x = y) == @ap _ _ (to_plus L O X) x y.

goals.conclusion: plus_path x y o to_plus L O (x = y) == ap (to_plus L O X)
plus_path x y o to_plus L O (x = y) == ap (to_plus L O X)

contents: intros p; unfold plus_path, to_plus, equiv_path_O, equiv_path_OO, path_OO.

goals.conclusion:
({|
   equiv_fun := O_rec (ap (to L (O_reflector O X)));
   equiv_isequiv := isequiv_path_OO L L (to O X x) (to O X y)
 |}
 oE equiv_O_functor L
      {|
        equiv_fun := O_rec (ap (to O X));
        equiv_isequiv := isequiv_path_OO O O x y
      |})
  (to L (O (x = y)) (to O (x = y) p)) =
ap (fun x0 : X => to L (O X) (to O X x0)) p

O_rec (ap (to (modality_subuniv L) (O_reflector (modality_subuniv O) X)))
  (O_functor L (O_rec (ap (to (modality_subuniv O) X)))
     (to (modality_subuniv L) (O (x = y)) (to (modality_subuniv O) (x = y) p))) =
ap
  (fun x0 : X => to (modality_subuniv L) (O X) (to (modality_subuniv O) X x0))
  p

O_rec (ap (to (modality_subuniv L) (O_reflector (modality_subuniv O) X)))
  (to L (to (modality_subuniv O) X x = to (modality_subuniv O) X y)
     (O_rec (ap (to (modality_subuniv O) X))
        (to (modality_subuniv O) (x = y) p))) =
ap
  (fun x0 : X => to (modality_subuniv L) (O X) (to (modality_subuniv O) X x0))
  p

ap (to (modality_subuniv L) (O_reflector (modality_subuniv O) X))
  (O_rec (ap (to (modality_subuniv O) X)) (to (modality_subuniv O) (x = y) p)) =
ap
  (fun x0 : X => to (modality_subuniv L) (O X) (to (modality_subuniv O) X x0))
  p

ap (to (modality_subuniv L) (O_reflector (modality_subuniv O) X))
  (O_rec (ap (to (modality_subuniv O) X)) (to (modality_subuniv O) (x = y) p)) =
ap (to L (O X)) (ap (to O X) p)

O_rec (ap (to (modality_subuniv O) X)) (to (modality_subuniv O) (x = y) p) =
ap (to O X) p

contents: (** This implies that plus-connected types are closed under path-spaces. *)

Definition plusconnected_path {X : Type} (x y : X)
             (pc : PlusConnected L O X) : PlusConnected L O (x = y).

goals.conclusion: PlusConnected L O (x = y)
PlusConnected L O (x = y)
NullHomotopy (to_plus L O (x = y))
NullHomotopy (fun x0 : x = y => plus_path x y (to_plus L O (x = y) x0))
NullHomotopy (ap (to_plus L O X))

contents: (** And hence plus-connected maps are closed under diagonals. *)

Definition plusconnmap_diagonal {X Y : Type} (f : X -> Y)
    : PlusConnMap L O f -> PlusConnMap L O (diagonal f).

goals.conclusion: PlusConnMap L O f -> PlusConnMap L O (diagonal f)
PlusConnMap L O f -> PlusConnMap L O (diagonal f)
PlusConnected L O (hfiber (diagonal f) p)
PlusConnected L O ((p.1; (p.2).2) = ((p.2).1; 1))

Definition plus_hfiber {X Y : Type} (f : X -> Y) (y : Y)
    : Plus L O (hfiber f y) <~> hfiber (plus_functor L O f) (to_plus L O Y y).

goals.conclusion: Plus L O (hfiber f y) <~> hfiber (plus_functor L O f) (to_plus L O Y y)

Definition plus_hfiber_to_plus {X Y : Type} (f : X -> Y) (y : Y)
    : plus_hfiber f y o to_plus L O (hfiber f y)
      == functor_hfiber (fun u => (to_plus_natural L O f u)^) y.

goals.conclusion:
plus_hfiber f y o to_plus L O (hfiber f y) ==
functor_hfiber (fun u : X => (to_plus_natural L O f u)^) y

plus_hfiber f y o to_plus L O (hfiber f y) ==
functor_hfiber (fun u : X => (to_plus_natural L O f u)^) y

(equiv_O_functor_hfiber L (O_functor O f) (to O Y y)
 oE equiv_O_functor L (equiv_O_functor_hfiber O f y))
  (to L (O (hfiber f y)) (to O (hfiber f y) (x; q))) =
functor_hfiber (fun u : X => (to_plus_natural L O f u)^) y (x; q)

O_functor_hfiber L (O_functor O f) (to (modality_subuniv O) Y y)
  (O_functor L (O_functor_hfiber O f y)
     (to (modality_subuniv L) (O (hfiber f y))
        (to (modality_subuniv O) (hfiber f y) (x; q)))) =
functor_hfiber (fun u : X => (to_plus_natural L O f u)^) y (x; q)

O_functor_hfiber L (O_functor O f) (to (modality_subuniv O) Y y)

     (O_functor_hfiber O f y (to (modality_subuniv O) (hfiber f y) (x; q)))) =
functor_hfiber (fun u : X => (to_plus_natural L O f u)^) y (x; q)

functor_hfiber (fun u : O X => (to_O_natural L (O_functor O f) u)^)
  (to (modality_subuniv O) Y y)
  (O_functor_hfiber O f y (to (modality_subuniv O) (hfiber f y) (x; q))) =
functor_hfiber (fun u : X => (to_plus_natural L O f u)^) y (x; q)

(to (modality_subuniv L) (O X)
   (O_rec
      (fun X0 : hfiber f y =>
       (to (modality_subuniv O) X X0.1;
       to_O_natural O f X0.1 @ ap (to (modality_subuniv O) Y) X0.2))
      (to (modality_subuniv O) (hfiber f y) (x; q))).1;
((to_O_natural L (O_functor O f)
    (O_rec

        (to (modality_subuniv O) X X0.1;
        to_O_natural O f X0.1 @ ap (to (modality_subuniv O) Y) X0.2))
       (to (modality_subuniv O) (hfiber f y) (x; q))).1)^)^ @
ap (to (modality_subuniv L) (O Y))
  (O_rec

      (to (modality_subuniv O) X X0.1;
      to_O_natural O f X0.1 @ ap (to (modality_subuniv O) Y) X0.2))
     (to (modality_subuniv O) (hfiber f y) (x; q))).2) =
(to (modality_subuniv L) (O X) (to (modality_subuniv O) X x);
((to_plus_natural L O f x)^)^ @
ap
  (fun x0 : Y => to (modality_subuniv L) (O Y) (to (modality_subuniv O) Y x0))
  q)

(to (modality_subuniv L) (O X) (to (modality_subuniv O) X x);
((to_O_natural L (O_functor O f) (to (modality_subuniv O) X x))^)^ @
ap (to (modality_subuniv L) (O Y))
  (to_O_natural O f x @ ap (to (modality_subuniv O) Y) q)) =
(to (modality_subuniv L) (O X) (to (modality_subuniv O) X x);
((to_plus_natural L O f x)^)^ @
ap
  (fun x0 : Y => to (modality_subuniv L) (O Y) (to (modality_subuniv O) Y x0))

((to_O_natural L (O_functor O f) (to (modality_subuniv O) X x))^)^ @
ap (to (modality_subuniv L) (O Y))
  (to_O_natural O f x @ ap (to (modality_subuniv O) Y) q) =
((to_plus_natural L O f x)^)^ @
ap
  (fun x0 : Y => to (modality_subuniv L) (O Y) (to (modality_subuniv O) Y x0))

((to_O_natural L (O_functor O f) (to (modality_subuniv O) X x))^)^ @
ap (to (modality_subuniv L) (O Y))

((to_O_natural L (O_functor O f) (to O X x) @
  ap (to L (O Y)) (to_O_natural O f x))^)^ @
ap
  (fun x0 : Y => to (modality_subuniv L) (O Y) (to (modality_subuniv O) Y x0))

 ap (to (modality_subuniv L) (O Y)) (to_O_natural O f x)) @
ap (to (modality_subuniv L) (O Y)) (ap (to (modality_subuniv O) Y) q) =
(to_O_natural L (O_functor O f) (to O X x) @
 ap (to L (O Y)) (to_O_natural O f x)) @
ap
  (fun x0 : Y => to (modality_subuniv L) (O Y) (to (modality_subuniv O) Y x0))

  (fun x0 : Y => to (modality_subuniv L) (O Y) (to (modality_subuniv O) Y x0))

ap

  q =
ap
  (fun x0 : Y => to (modality_subuniv L) (O Y) (to (modality_subuniv O) Y x0))

Definition equiv_plus_pullback {A B C : Type} (f : B -> A) (g : C -> A)
    : Plus L O (Pullback f g) <~> Pullback (plus_functor L O f) (plus_functor L O g).

goals.conclusion:
Plus L O (Pullback f g) <~>
Pullback (plus_functor L O f) (plus_functor L O g)

Plus L O (Pullback f g) <~>
Pullback (plus_functor L O f) (plus_functor L O g)

contents: refine (equiv_O_pullback L (O_functor O f) (O_functor O g) oE _).

goals.conclusion: Plus L O (Pullback f g) <~> L (Pullback (O_functor O f) (O_functor O g))
O (Pullback f g) <~> Pullback (O_functor O f) (O_functor O g)

contents:
Definition diagonal_plus_functor {A B : Type} (f : A -> B)
    : diagonal (plus_functor L O f) == equiv_plus_pullback f f o plus_functor L O (diagonal f).

goals.conclusion:
diagonal (plus_functor L O f) ==
equiv_plus_pullback f f o plus_functor L O (diagonal f)

diagonal (plus_functor L O f) ==
equiv_plus_pullback f f o plus_functor L O (diagonal f)

diagonal (plus_functor L O f) x =
equiv_plus_pullback f f (plus_functor L O (diagonal f) x)

equiv_O_pullback L (O_functor O f) (O_functor O f)
  (O_functor L (diagonal (O_functor O f)) x) =
equiv_plus_pullback f f (plus_functor L O (diagonal f) x)

contents: apply (ap (equiv_O_pullback L (O_functor O f) (O_functor O f))).

goals.conclusion:
O_functor L (diagonal (O_functor O f)) x =
equiv_O_functor L (equiv_O_pullback O f f) (plus_functor L O (diagonal f) x)

O_functor L
  (fun x0 : O A => equiv_O_pullback O f f (O_functor O (diagonal f) x0)) x =
equiv_O_functor L (equiv_O_pullback O f f) (plus_functor L O (diagonal f) x)

O_functor L
  (fun x0 : O A => equiv_O_pullback O f f (O_functor O (diagonal f) x0)) x =
equiv_O_functor L (equiv_O_pullback O f f)
  (O_functor L (O_functor O (diagonal f)) x)

contents: (** Recall that a modality is characterized by connectedness of the units.  Analogously, we can now prove that the plus-units are all plus-connected.  This is equivalently a sort of coherence axiom for the homotopy [wellpointed_plus], that when precomposed with [to_plus] it becomes [to_plus_natural]. *)
Definition plusconnmap_to_plus (X : Type) : PlusConnMap L O (to_plus L O X).

goals.conclusion: PlusConnMap L O (to_plus L O X)
PlusConnMap L O (to_plus L O X)
NullHomotopy (to_plus L O (hfiber (to_plus L O X) y))

NullHomotopy
  (fun x : hfiber (to_plus L O X) y =>
   plus_hfiber (to_plus L O X) y (to_plus L O (hfiber (to_plus L O X) y) x))

contents: apply (nullhomotopy_homotopic (fun u => (plus_hfiber_to_plus (to_plus L O X) y u)^)).

goals.conclusion:
NullHomotopy
  (functor_hfiber (fun u : X => (to_plus_natural L O (to_plus L O X) u)^) y)

{y0
: {x : Plus L O X &
  plus_functor L O (to_plus L O X) x = to_plus L O (Plus L O X) y}
&
forall x : {x : X & to_plus L O X x = y},

y0}
plus_functor L O (to_plus L O X) y = to_plus L O (Plus L O X) y

forall x : {x : X & to_plus L O X x = y},
functor_hfiber (fun u : X => (to_plus_natural L O (to_plus L O X) u)^) y x =
(y; ?Goal)
plus_functor L O (to_plus L O X) y = to_plus L O (Plus L O X) y

forall x : {x : X & to_plus L O X x = y},
functor_hfiber (fun u : X => (to_plus_natural L O (to_plus L O X) u)^) y x =
(y; (wellpointed_plus L O X y)^)

functor_hfiber (fun u : X => (to_plus_natural L O (to_plus L O X) u)^)
  (to_plus L O X x) (x; 1) =
(to_plus L O X x; (wellpointed_plus L O X (to_plus L O X x))^)

(to_plus L O X x; ((to_plus_natural L O (to_plus L O X) x)^)^ @ 1) =
(to_plus L O X x; (wellpointed_plus L O X (to_plus L O X x))^)

((to_plus_natural L O (to_plus L O X) x)^)^ @ 1 =
(wellpointed_plus L O X (to_plus L O X x))^

to_plus_natural L O (to_plus L O X) x =
(wellpointed_plus L O X (to_plus L O X x))^

to_plus_natural L O (to_plus L O X) x =
(O_indpaths (to_plus L O (Plus L O X)) (plus_functor L O (to_plus L O X))
   (fun ox : O X =>
    ap (to L (O (Plus L O X)))
      (O_indpaths
         (fun x0 : O_reflector O X => to O (Plus L O X) (to L (O X) x0))
         (O_functor O (fun x0 : X => to L (O X) (to O X x0)))
         (fun x0 : X =>
          (to_O_natural O (fun x1 : X => to L (O X) (to O X x1)) x0)^)
         ox) @
    (to_O_natural L (O_functor O (fun x0 : X => to L (O X) (to O X x0))) ox)^)
   (to_plus L O X x))^

to_plus_natural L O (to_plus L O X) x =
(ap (to L (O (Plus L O X)))
   (to_O_natural O (fun x0 : X => to L (O X) (to O X x0)) x)^ @
 (to_O_natural L (O_functor O (fun x0 : X => to L (O X) (to O X x0)))
    (to O X x))^)^

to_plus_natural L O (to_plus L O X) x =
to_O_natural L (O_functor O (fun x0 : X => to L (O X) (to O X x0)))
  (to O X x) @
ap (to L (O (Plus L O X)))
  (to_O_natural O (fun x0 : X => to L (O X) (to O X x0)) x)

contents: (** Recall also (from [nsep_iff_trunc_to_O]) that a type is n-separated for a lex modality [O] if and only if its [O]-unit is an n-truncated map.  We can now prove the analogous fact for the plus-construction.  We state this using [MapIn (Tr n)] instead of [IsTrunc n] because we have more useful lemmas for [MapIn]. *)

Definition nsep_iff_trunc_plus (n : trunc_index) (X : Type)
    : In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X).

goals.conclusion: In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X)
In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X)

contents: revert X; induction n as [|n IHn]; intros X; split; intros H.

goals.conclusion: MapIn (Tr (-2)) (to_plus L O X)
In (nSep (-2) (Meet L O)) X
MapIn (Tr n.+1) (to_plus L O X)

hypotheses.type: forall X0 : Type, In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X0 <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X0)

goals.conclusion: In (nSep n.+1 (Meet L O)) X

hypotheses.type: forall X0 : Type, In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X0 <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X0)

goals.conclusion: MapIn (Tr (-2)) (to_plus L O X)
IsEquiv (to_plus L O X)
In (nSep (-2) (Meet L O)) X
IsEquiv (to_plus L O X)
MapIn (Tr n.+1) (to_plus L O X)

hypotheses.type: forall X0 : Type, In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X0 <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X0)

goals.conclusion: IsTruncMap n (ap (to_plus L O X))

hypotheses.type: forall X0 : Type, In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X0 <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X0)

goals.conclusion: MapIn (Tr n) (ap (to_plus L O X))

hypotheses.type: forall X0 : Type, In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X0 <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X0)

goals.conclusion: MapIn (Tr n) (ap (to_plus L O X))

hypotheses.type: forall X0 : Type, In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X0 <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X0)

goals.conclusion: In (nSep n.+1 (Meet L O)) X

hypotheses.type: forall X0 : Type, In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X0 <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X0)

goals.conclusion: In (nSep n (Meet L O)) (x = y)

hypotheses.type: forall X0 : Type, In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X0 <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X0)

goals.conclusion: MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O (x = y))

hypotheses.type: forall X0 : Type, In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X0 <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X0)

contents: pose (i := istruncmap_ap n (to_plus L O X) x y).

goals.conclusion: MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O (x = y))

hypotheses.type: forall X0 : Type, In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X0 <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X0)

hypotheses.body: istruncmap_ap n (to_plus L O X) x y

goals.conclusion: MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O (x = y))

hypotheses.type: forall X0 : Type, In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X0 <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X0)

contents: apply (mapinO_homotopic _ ((plus_path x y)^-1 o (@ap _ _ (to_plus L O X) x y))).

goals.conclusion:
(fun x0 : x = y => (plus_path x y)^-1 (ap (to_plus L O X) x0)) ==
to_plus L O (x = y)

hypotheses.type: forall X0 : Type, In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X0 <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X0)

goals.conclusion: MapIn (Tr n) (fun x0 : x = y => (plus_path x y)^-1 (ap (to_plus L O X) x0))

hypotheses.type: forall X0 : Type, In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X0 <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X0)

goals.conclusion:
(fun x0 : x = y => (plus_path x y)^-1 (ap (to_plus L O X) x0)) ==
to_plus L O (x = y)

hypotheses.type: forall X0 : Type, In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X0 <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X0)

contents: intros p; apply moveR_equiv_V; symmetry; apply plus_path_to_plus.

goals.conclusion: MapIn (Tr n) (fun x0 : x = y => (plus_path x y)^-1 (ap (to_plus L O X) x0))

hypotheses.type: forall X0 : Type, In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X0 <-> MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O X0)

contents: (** We now make one more assumption, that the plus-construction inverts plus-connected embeddings.  In the case of the plus-construction for stacks, this corresponds roughly to the "local character" condition on a Grothendieck topology. *)

Context (composing : forall (X Y : Type) (f : X -> Y)
                              (fe : IsEmbedding f) (fc : PlusConnMap L O f),
              IsEquiv (plus_functor L O f)).
(** This implies, by induction, that the plus-construction decreases the truncation-level of any finitely truncated plus-connected map. *)

Definition istruncmap_plus_functor {n : trunc_index} {X Y : Type} (f : X -> Y)
             `{MapIn (Tr n.+1) _ _ f} (pc : PlusConnMap L O f)
    : MapIn (Tr n) (plus_functor L O f).

goals.conclusion: MapIn (Tr n) (plus_functor L O f)

hypotheses.type:
forall (X0 Y0 : Type) (f0 : X0 -> Y0),
IsEmbedding f0 -> PlusConnMap L O f0 -> IsEquiv (plus_functor L O f0)

goals.conclusion: MapIn (Tr n) (plus_functor L O f)

hypotheses.type:
forall (X0 Y0 : Type) (f0 : X0 -> Y0),
IsEmbedding f0 -> PlusConnMap L O f0 -> IsEquiv (plus_functor L O f0)

contents: generalize dependent f; revert X Y; induction n as [|n IHn]; intros X Y f ? pc.

   equiv_plus_pullback f f (plus_functor L O (diagonal f) u))

contents: (** It follows, by applying this to the plus-unit and using well-pointedness, that the plus-construction on *types* decreases their plus-separatedness. *)

Definition nsep_plus (n : trunc_index) (X : Type) `{In (nSep n.+1 (Meet L O)) X}
    : In (nSep n (Meet L O)) (Plus L O X).

goals.conclusion: In (nSep n (Meet L O)) (Plus L O X)

hypotheses.type:
forall (X0 Y : Type) (f : X0 -> Y),
IsEmbedding f -> PlusConnMap L O f -> IsEquiv (plus_functor L O f)

goals.conclusion: In (nSep n (Meet L O)) (Plus L O X)

hypotheses.type:
forall (X0 Y : Type) (f : X0 -> Y),
IsEmbedding f -> PlusConnMap L O f -> IsEquiv (plus_functor L O f)

goals.conclusion: MapIn (Tr n) (to_plus L O (Plus L O X))

hypotheses.type:
forall (X0 Y : Type) (f : X0 -> Y),
IsEmbedding f -> PlusConnMap L O f -> IsEquiv (plus_functor L O f)

contents: nrefine (mapinO_homotopic _ _ (fun u => (wellpointed_plus L O X u)^)).

goals.conclusion: MapIn (Tr n) (plus_functor L O (to_plus L O X))

forall (X0 Y : Type) (f : X0 -> Y),
IsEmbedding f -> PlusConnMap L O f -> IsEquiv (plus_functor L O f)

IsEmbedding f -> PlusConnMap L O f -> IsEquiv (plus_functor L O f)

forall (X0 Y : Type) (f : X0 -> Y),
IsEmbedding f -> PlusConnMap L O f -> IsEquiv (plus_functor L O f)

contents: (** Therefore, if a type starts out as n-plus-separated, then n+2 applications of the plus-construction suffice to make it (-2)-plus-separated, i.e. in the meet subuniverse.  Hence it has a reflection. *)

#[export] Instance prereflects_plus_nsep (n : trunc_index) (X : Type) `{In (nSep n (Meet L O)) X}
    : PreReflects (Meet L O) X.

goals.conclusion: PreReflects (Meet L O) X

hypotheses.type:
forall (X0 Y : Type) (f : X0 -> Y),
IsEmbedding f -> PlusConnMap L O f -> IsEquiv (plus_functor L O f)

IsEmbedding f -> PlusConnMap L O f -> IsEquiv (plus_functor L O f)
